text_blocks.text: Elsewhere in the Americas, in the Caribbean in
particular, Baptist missionaries took an active role in the
anti-slavery movement. In Jamaica, for example, William
Knibb, a prominent British Baptist missionary, worked toward
the emancipation of slaves in the British West Indies (which
took place in 1838). Knibb also protagonist the creation of
"Free Villages"; rural communities centred around a Baptist
church where emancipated slaves could farm their own land.
Baptists were likewise active in promoting the education of
former slaves; for example, Jamaica's Calabar High School, named
after the slave port of Calabar, was formed by Baptist
missionaries. At the same time, during and after slavery,
slaves and free formed their own Spiritual Baptist movements
- breakaway spiritual movements which often expressed
